journey.title: First line of Python
journey.text: Automation scripts for mundane tasks. Discovered that code could buy back time — and curiosity.

journey.title: Backend as craft
journey.text: APIs, databases, and the quiet satisfaction of systems that just work. Django and FastAPI became home.

journey.title: Into intelligence
journey.text: Machine learning stopped being a buzzword and became a toolkit — models, pipelines, and responsible design.

journey.title: Systems + stories
journey.text: Building products by day. Collecting mountains, light, and journals by weekend. Both feed the work.

lessons: Ship the smallest honest version first.
Documentation is kindness to your future self.
Nature resets the quality of thinking.
Constraints are creative fuel, not cages.
beliefs: Software should feel invisible when it works.
Adventure is research for better judgement.
howIWork: Start with the problem, not the framework.
Prefer boring technology for critical paths.
Write for the next engineer — including yourself.
Protect deep work like a calendar ritual.

workflow.detail: Clear inbox, one priority, coffee, deep focus block.
Implement, test, commit small. Prefer vertical slices.
Read the diff as a stranger. Simplify ruthlessly.
Walk, train, or shoot. Let the subconscious compile.

goals: Ship production AI systems with clear evaluation loops.
Master systems design for high-throughput APIs.
Document one mountain journey with full photo stories.
vision: A life where engineering precision and outdoor wonder reinforce each other — building calm, durable software while staying close to mountains, cameras, and continuous learning.

books.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
books.author: Martin Kleppmann
books.note: The systems bible — still reread chapters annually.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.note: Compounding applies to code and climbing alike.

books.title: The Creative Act
books.author: Rick Rubin
books.note: Permission to trust taste over noise.

workspace: Minimal desk, warm lamp, one large monitor
Notebook always open for architecture sketches
Standing sessions between deep focus blocks
